exercises.name: Marching on the spot
exercises.cue: Knees high, arms swinging. Just wake the system up.

exercises.name: Leg swings — forward/back
exercises.cue: Hand on wall. Control the range, don't ballistic it. Hip should feel like it's loosening, not pulling.

exercises.name: Leg swings — lateral
exercises.cue: Face the wall, swing across body. Opens adductors and abductors dynamically.

exercises.name: 90/90 hip switches
exercises.cue: Front leg 90° in front, back leg 90° to side. Rotate side to side. Slow. This unlocks internal and external rotation — what most rugby players are tight in.

exercises.name: World's greatest stretch
exercises.cue: Lunge, hand inside front foot. Rotate up to ceiling. Hits hip flexor, T-spine, and hamstring in one.

exercises.name: Cossack squat
exercises.cue: Wide stance, shift side to side. Sit into one hip. Shallow first, deeper as warm. KEY for adductors — load through range, don't passively stretch.

exercises.name: Jefferson curl
exercises.cue: Bodyweight only or very light. Roll down vertebra by vertebra, hang, roll up. Loads posterior chain gently through full range.

exercises.name: Active straight leg raise
exercises.cue: On back, lift one leg straight up, control down. No yanking to end range. You're owning the range, not stretching into it.

exercises.name: Glute bridges
exercises.cue: 2 sec pause at top. Drive through heels. If you feel hamstrings cramping you're not using glutes — tuck pelvis first.

exercises.name: Single-leg glute bridge
exercises.cue: One foot down, other leg extended or knee to chest. Glute on standing leg does the work. Pause at top.

exercises.name: Long-lever bridge isometric
exercises.cue: Heels far from body, legs nearly straight. Hold bridge at top. Loads hamstring without compressing the tendon — your safe entry for proximal hamstring work.

exercises.name: Copenhagen plank
exercises.cue: Top leg on couch/bench, hold side plank. Knee on bench first, foot once easy. Best single exercise for adductor resilience.

exercises.name: Single-leg RDL
exercises.cue: Slow, bodyweight or light DBs. Hinge from hip, back leg straight behind. Loads hamstring attachment eccentrically and trains balance.

exercises.name: Deep squat hold
exercises.cue: Elbows into knees, push knees out, open hips. Chest up.

exercises.name: Standing forward fold
exercises.cue: Soft knees, just hang. Don't reach for floor — let gravity do it.

exercises.name: Couch stretch
exercises.cue: Back foot up on couch/wall, front leg in lunge. Squeeze glute on back leg to open hip flexor properly.

exercises.name: Pigeon stretch
exercises.cue: Front leg bent in front of body, back leg straight behind. Sink hips down. External rotation and glute med.

exercises.name: Thread the needle
exercises.cue: On all fours, slide one arm under the other, rotate T-spine. Forehead to floor.

exercises.name: Open book
exercises.cue: Side lying, knees stacked, top arm rotates open to other side. Eyes follow hand. Pure T-spine rotation.

exercises.name: Cat-cow
exercises.cue: Slow. Articulate each segment. Spine warm-up, not a yoga performance.

exercises.name: Child's pose
exercises.cue: Knees wide, hips back to heels, arms long. Breathe into the back.

exercises.name: Scapular push-ups
exercises.cue: Plank position, arms locked. Just protract and retract shoulder blades. Wakes up serratus.

exercises.name: Band pull-aparts
exercises.cue: Light band, arms straight, pull apart at chest height. Squeeze shoulder blades. Counters the hunched-forward training day.

exercises.name: Wall slides
exercises.cue: Back against wall, arms in goalpost. Slide up and down keeping contact. Hard if your T-spine is locked.

exercises.name: Ankle rocks
exercises.cue: Half-kneeling, drive front knee forward over toes. Keep heel down. Dorsiflexion for squat and sprint depth.

exercises.name: Calf raises
exercises.cue: Slow up, slow down, full range. Achilles and posterior chain prep.

exercises.name: A-skips
exercises.cue: Drive knee up, opposite arm. Tall posture, foot strikes under hip. Sprint mechanics primer.

exercises.name: B-skips
exercises.cue: A-skip but with a leg cycle out — paw the ground back. Hamstring activation, sprint-specific.

exercises.name: High knees
exercises.cue: Quick feet, knees to hip height. Wakes CNS, raises HR.

exercises.name: Pogo jumps
exercises.cue: Stiff ankles, bounce on balls of feet. Minimal ground contact time. Reactive strength primer.

exercises.name: Sub-max broad jumps
exercises.cue: About 70%. Stick the landing. CNS prep, not a max effort.

exercises.name: Lateral bounds
exercises.cue: Push off outside leg, land on opposite, stick it. Hip stability and change of direction primer.

exercises.name: Spiderman lunge
exercises.cue: Step into deep lunge, drop opposite hand inside front foot. Hip mobility, T-spine if you reach up.

exercises.name: Inchworm
exercises.cue: Bend, walk hands out to plank, walk feet back to hands. Hamstring length plus core.

exercises.name: Frog stretch
exercises.cue: On all fours, knees wide, rock back. Adductor and hip mobility. Don't force it.

exercises.name: Adductor rockback
exercises.cue: Half-kneeling, one leg straight out to side, rock hips back over down knee. Loaded stretch for adductor.

exercises.name: Copenhagen isometric (knee)
exercises.cue: Side plank with knee on bench, top leg. Hold. Easier version — start here if foot-on-bench is too much.

exercises.name: Side plank
exercises.cue: Stack hips. Don't sag. Lateral chain.

exercises.name: Dead bug
exercises.cue: Lower back pressed flat. Opposite arm and leg extend slow. Anti-extension core.

exercises.name: Bird dog
exercises.cue: All fours, opposite arm and leg extend. Hold 2 sec. Don't rotate hips.

exercises.name: Monster walks
exercises.cue: Band around knees or ankles. Walk forward, sideways. Glute med activation.

exercises.name: Clamshells
exercises.cue: Side lying, band optional. Open knee, keep feet together, don't roll back. Glute med isolation.

exercises.name: Tib raises
exercises.cue: Back against wall, lift toes up. Shin work — undertrained, helps sprint and change of direction.

exercises.name: Ankle circles
exercises.cue: Both directions. Bored but useful.

exercises.name: Thoracic extension on roller
exercises.cue: Foam roller under mid-back, hands behind head, extend back over it. Move roller up an inch, repeat. Counters scrum/seated posture.

exercises.name: Shin box getup
exercises.cue: From 90/90 position, rotate up onto front knee, then back down to other side. Hip rotational strength.

exercises.name: Box breathing
exercises.cue: 4 in, 4 hold, 4 out, 4 hold. Down-regulate. Evening or pre-sleep.

exercises.name: Supine spinal twist
exercises.cue: On back, one knee across body, opposite shoulder stays down. Just breathe.

exercises.name: Legs up the wall
exercises.cue: Lie on back, legs vertical up the wall. Down-regulate. Drains the legs.

exercises.name: Hip flexor isometric hold
exercises.cue: Half-kneeling, squeeze back glute, hold. Active hip flexor opener — beats passive lunging.

exercises.name: Split squat isometric
exercises.cue: Half-depth split squat, hold. Builds unilateral leg tolerance.

exercises.name: Nordic curl (assisted)
exercises.cue: Kneel, feet anchored, lower forward slow. Push back up with arms as needed. Eccentric hamstring strength — gold standard.

exercises.name: Neck circles
exercises.cue: Slow, controlled, both directions. Rugby necks need it.

exercises.name: Wrist rolls and stretches
exercises.cue: Circles, then palms together prayer, then back of hands together. Passing and ball-handling need wrist health.
